message:
feat(fnp-python): numpy.random module-level legacy API

Install a singleton RandomState as fnp_python.random._rand at module
init and bind every available distribution / seeding method onto the
module namespace, mirroring numpy.random's legacy top-level aliases.

Implementation: at module init, run a Python helper that iterates the
numpy.random alias names (seed, get_state, set_state, rand, randn,
randint, random, random_sample, ranf, sample, random_integers,
tomaxint, bytes, normal, standard_normal, exponential, gamma, beta,
chisquare, f, geometric, gumbel, laplace, logistic, lognormal, pareto,
power, rayleigh, standard_cauchy, standard_exponential, standard_gamma,
standard_t, triangular, uniform, weibull, zipf) and `setattr(mod, name,
getattr(_rand, name))` for each — same pattern numpy uses internally.

Distributions not yet on PyRandomState (binomial, poisson, multinomial,
dirichlet, hypergeometric, logseries, multivariate_normal,
negative_binomial, noncentral_chisquare, noncentral_f, vonmises, wald,
choice, shuffle, permutation) are filtered via hasattr so they bind
automatically once added in a follow-up bead.

Extended random_namespace_matches_numpy_bit_generator_oracles to cover
40+ alias names plus bit-exact parity against numpy.random.rand /
standard_normal / randint after a shared seed(7). 442/442 tests green.

Part of franken_numpy-lxmb.
br-franken_numpy-t44q
